title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Automation and Data Client Systems · Spiral Review
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for redirect semantics, method rewriting, credential scope, and loop control, producing auditable redirect policy engine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers auditable redirect policy engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Redirect Status Semantics · changed case · Review Redirect Status Semantics
steps.instruction:
review case — compliance handoff automation sends POST P-5 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
